ol.li: Memory Churn: Random access pattern prevents CPU prefetching.
I/O Latency: Each vector write requires a separate I/O request.
Overhead: Lock contention is high under parallel workloads.
Lock Amortization: Cost of lock distributed across 1000 items (1/N).
Sequential Blit: Contiguous writes trigger CPU prefetchers and write-combining.
Throughput: Saturates NVMe bandwidth by reducing command overhead.
Cache Locality: Bulk metadata update keeps index nodes in L2/L3 cache.

div.p: Throughput: ~850 MB/s Random DRAM row activation delays (tRP, tRCD) dominate.
Throughput: ~42 GB/s Sequential access utilizes Burst Mode and prefetch circuitry.
